title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted where necessary

input_row.title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted where necessary
input_row.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
input_row.rationale: Mounting service account tokens inside pods can provide an avenue for privilege escalation attacks where an attacker is able to compromise a single pod in the cluster. Avoiding mounting these tokens removes this attack avenue.
input_row.audit: Review pod and service account objects in the cluster and ensure automatically mounting the service account token is disabled ( automountServiceAccountToken: false ), unless the resource explicitly requires this access. Find all pods that automatically mount service account tokens: oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.automountServiceAccountToken) | .metadata.name' Find all service accounts that automatically mount service tokens: oc get serviceaccounts -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.automountServiceAccountToken) | .metadata.name'
input_row.remediation: Modify the definition of pods and service accounts which do not need to mount service account tokens to disable it.
input_row.impact: Pods mounted without service account tokens will not be able to communicate with the API server, except where the resource is available to unauthenticated principals.
input_row.default_value: By default, all pods get a service account token mounted in them.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit approach is categorized as automated because:
  - The control can be verified using the IBM Cloud CLI commands.
  - The audit section provides specific commands to check the service account token configurations in OpenShift.
  - The required properties can be queried through the IBM Cloud APIs or CLI.
  - The checks pertain to standard Kubernetes and OpenShift practices which are supported by IBM Cloud services.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_pod_service_account_token_mount_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **API Calls:** Use IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service APIs to query the pod and service account configurations.
- **CLI Commands:**
  - To find all pods with automatically mounted service account tokens:
    ```bash
    oc get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.automountServiceAccountToken) | .metadata.name'
    ```
  - To find all service accounts with automatically mounted service tokens:
    ```bash
    oc get serviceaccounts -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.automountServiceAccountToken) | .metadata.name'
    ```
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Kubernetes clusters, namespaces, pods, and service accounts within the IBM Cloud Kubernetes service.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Ensure `automountServiceAccountToken` is set to `false` for all pods and service accounts unless explicitly required by the application.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - Pass: If there are no pods or service accounts found with `automountServiceAccountToken: true`.
  - Fail: If any pods or service accounts are found with `automountServiceAccountToken: true`, indicating a potential security risk related to privilege escalation.
